Title: ELSA-2026-8096:  perl:5.32 security update (MODERATE)

perl
[4:5.32.1-474]
- Resolves: RHEL-153834
- Fix CVE-2025-40909 - Clone dirhandles without fchdir

[4:5.32.1-473]
- Fix CVE-2023-47038
- Added perl-autouse and perl-ExtUtils-MM-Utils to perl run-requires

[4:5.32.1-472]
- Add definition of OPTIMIZE to .ph files, if optimizing is used
  (bug#2159760)

[4:5.32.1-471]
- XSLoader requires DynaLoader

[4:5.32.1-470]
- Updated list of *.ph files (bug#1972637)

[4:5.32.1-469]
- Fix dumping a hash entry of PL_strtab type
- Fix an arithmetic left shift of a minimal integer value (GH#18639)

[4:5.32.1-468]
- Protect locale tests from LANGUAGE environment variable
- Prevent the number of buckets in a hash from getting too large
- Fix a memory leak when compiling a regular expression (GH#18604)

[4:5.32.1-467]
- Fix a crash in optimizing split() (GH#18232)
- Make accessing environment by DynaLoader thread-safe
- Use duplocale() if available
- Fix fc() in Turkish locale
- Fix croaking on 'my /usr/bin/sh' when 'use utf8' is in effect (GH#18449)
- Fix PERL_UNUSED_ARG() definition in XSUB.h
- Add missing entries to perldiag (GH#18276)

[2.150010-1]
- Update to 2.150010
  Added:
  - Merged Parse::CPAN::Meta 1.4420 into this distribution
  Fixed:
  - CPAN::Meta::Prereqs now fully accepts phases and types starting with 'x_';
    new 'phases' and 'types_in' interfaces have been added
  - No longer relies on JSON backend for data structure cloning; this is much
    faster than using JSON::PP
  - The cloning routine would raise an error on expected types when it
    previously would stringify; the old behavior is restored
  - Fixed used of Encode in Parse::CPAN::Meta::load_json_string (cherry picked
    from Parse::CPAN::Meta 1.4422)
  - Added 'use warnings' to Parse::CPAN::Meta
  - The YAML and JSON backend variables are ignored when building/testing the
    perl core itself, where non-core backends are not yet installed
  Tests:
  - The 'extra_mappings' feature for meta merging is now tested and documented
  - During tests, delete new environment variables added by Parse::CPAN::Meta
    1.4418
  Spec:
  - Clarifies acceptable values for booleans
  - Cleaned up text and links of historical specs
- Obsolete old perl-Parse-CPAN-Meta package
- Simplify find command using -delete
